mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding economic activity and rising consumer income, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of months when PAYEMS is increasing.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 0.25 percent, emergency monetary policy suppresses discount rates and borrowing costs, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 55 percent of instances when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad demand expansion that lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increasing month-over-month
failure_condition: Rank information coefficient between the interaction term and 60-day returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-unemployment environment, a month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate signals labor market healing that reduces recession tail risk and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in less than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE declines while the unemployment rate is above 6 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production compresses default risk in cyclical sectors and supports positive earnings revisions, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between month-over-month INDPRO changes and 20-day S&P 500 returns is negative over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Headline consumer price inflation stable between 1.0 and 2.5 percent year-over-year reduces monetary policy uncertainty and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of months when CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation is between 1.0 and 2.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of a federal funds rate below 0.25 percent and a declining unemployment rate reflects a Goldilocks macro configuration where monetary stimulus supports growth without inflationary pressure, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: zero lower bound with labor market healing
failure_condition: Walk-forward rank IC of the FEDFUNDS and UNRATE interaction term remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1 million units signals durable demand strength in interest-rate-sensitive sectors, which drives cyclical equity outperformance and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 50 percent of triggered instances when HOUST exceeds 1000 units.
